title: Guardian Vulnerability Queue
description: Schema for vulnerability queue files produced by vuln skills and consumed by exploit skills.

vulnerabilities.description: List of vulnerabilities discovered during analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the vulnerability (e.g. INJ-001, XSS-003).

vulnerability_type.description: Category of vulnerability (e.g. sql_injection, xss, auth_bypass, ssrf, authz).

externally_exploitable.description: Whether the vulnerability is reachable from an external attacker.

notes.description: Optional context or analyst notes about the vulnerability.

source.description: Injection domain: taint source where untrusted data enters.

path.description: Injection/XSS domain: data-flow path from source to sink.

sink_call.description: Injection domain: the dangerous function or query call.

slot_type.description: Injection domain: type of slot the tainted data fills (e.g. value, column, table).

sanitization_observed.description: Injection domain: any sanitization or escaping observed on the path.

concat_occurrences.description: Injection domain: number of string-concatenation occurrences in the query.

witness_payload.description: Injection/XSS domain: proof-of-concept payload demonstrating the vulnerability.

verdict.description: Injection/XSS domain: final verdict on exploitability.

mismatch_reason.description: Injection/XSS domain: reason if the finding was downgraded or reclassified.

source_detail.description: XSS domain: detailed source information for the tainted input.

sink_function.description: XSS domain: the rendering or DOM-manipulation function acting as a sink.

render_context.description: XSS domain: the HTML/JS context where the payload is rendered.

encoding_observed.description: XSS domain: any output encoding observed before rendering.

source_endpoint.description: Auth/SSRF domain: the HTTP endpoint where the vulnerability is exposed.

vulnerable_code_location.description: Auth/SSRF/Authz domain: file and line location of the vulnerable code.

missing_defense.description: Auth/SSRF domain: the security control that is absent or misconfigured.

exploitation_hypothesis.description: Auth/SSRF domain: hypothesis for how an attacker could exploit this.

suggested_exploit_technique.description: Auth domain: recommended technique for exploiting the vulnerability.

vulnerable_parameter.description: SSRF domain: the request parameter susceptible to SSRF.

endpoint.description: Authz domain: the API endpoint affected by the authorization flaw.

role_context.description: Authz domain: the role or permission context relevant to the finding.

guard_evidence.description: Authz domain: evidence of existing (insufficient) authorization guards.

side_effect.description: Authz domain: observable side effect proving the authorization bypass.

minimal_witness.description: Authz domain: minimal request or sequence that demonstrates the bypass.
